11.1.title: मोहोऽयं विगतः — delusion lifted by speech
11.1.body: मदनुग्रहाय is चतुर्थी एकवचन ("for my sake"), framing chapters 7–10 as a personal favour. विगतः is past passive participle agreeing with मोहः (both प्रथमा एकवचन पुल्लिंग) — the finite verb is suppressed, the state itself is the report.

11.1.title: गुह्यम् + अध्यात्म-संज्ञितम् — the secret named
11.1.body: गुह्यम् is a neuter substantive ("the secret"), and अध्यात्म-संज्ञितम् glosses it: that-which-is-named ‘अध्यात्म’. यत् ... तेन is the relative–correlative skeleton: the discourse is the instrumental cause, मोह the patient, विगतः the verbal adjective.

11.2.title: भव-अप्ययौ — द्विवचन pair of origin and dissolution
11.2.body: भव-अप्ययौ is a द्वन्द्व compound in द्विवचन ("arising-and-perishing"), the two book-ends of every भूत. श्रुतौ is past passive participle, द्विवचन agreeing with भवाप्ययौ, with मया as the तृतीया agent ("heard by me").

11.2.title: कमल-पत्र-अक्ष — vocative as breath-pause
11.2.body: कमल-पत्र-अक्ष is सम्बोधन — a बहुव्रीहि ("he-whose-eyes-are-lotus-petals") inserted mid-verse. माहात्म्यम् ... अव्ययम् pairs noun and adjective in नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा, both governed by श्रुतम् understood from श्रुतौ.

11.3.title: यथा आत्थ — quoting Krishna back at himself
11.3.body: आत्थ is लट् मध्यम पुरुष एकवचन of √ब्रू ("thou sayest"), one of Sanskrit's archaic conjugated forms. यथा आत्थ त्वम् आत्मानम् — "in the manner thou describest thyself" — closes the loop: Krishna's words become Arjuna's premise.

11.3.title: द्रष्टुम् इच्छामि — infinitive of desire
11.3.body: द्रष्टुम् is the infinitive (तुमुन्) of √दृश्, governed by इच्छामि (लट् उत्तम एकवचन of √इष्). The infinitive does not anchor the sentence; इच्छामि does. ऐश्वरम् रूपम् is the द्वितीया object, vocative पुरुषोत्तम stands apart.

11.4.title: मन्यसे यदि — conditional courtesy
11.4.body: मन्यसे is लट् मध्यम पुरुष एकवचन आत्मनेपद of √मन् ("if you think"). शक्यम् is the predicative adjective with implicit अस्ति. Arjuna is grammatically deferential: only after Krishna's permission may the seeing happen.

11.4.title: दर्शय आत्मानम् — imperative + reflexive object
11.4.body: दर्शय is the causative imperative ("cause to be seen") of √दृश्. आत्मानम् is द्वितीया एकवचन — Krishna is asked to show his own self. अव्ययम् agrees with आत्मानम् in case, number, and gender (पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन).

11.5.title: पश्य — the imperative that starts the vision
11.5.body: पश्य is लोट् मध्यम पुरुष एकवचन of √दृश् — the single command that opens the cosmic chapter. शतशः and सहस्रशः are distributive adverbs in -शस् suffix, modifying रूपाणि (नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन).

11.5.title: नाना-विध / नाना-वर्ण-आकृति — manifold modifiers
11.5.body: Both are बहुव्रीहि / तत्पुरुष compounds qualifying रूपाणि, all नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन — perfect सामानाधिकरण्य chain. दिव्यानि ("divine") is the load-bearing adjective: these are not the rūpas of the नरलोक.

11.6.title: पश्य आदित्यान् वसून् रुद्रान् — four-fold catalogue
11.6.body: Each accusative — आदित्यान्, वसून्, रुद्रान्, अश्विनौ, मरुतः — is governed by the single imperative पश्य, the verb absorbing the whole pantheon. अश्विनौ stands out as द्विवचन, the twin gods grammatically refusing the plural.

11.6.title: अदृष्ट-पूर्वाणि — the never-before-seen
11.6.body: अदृष्ट-पूर्वाणि is a बहुव्रीहि ("those-whose-prior-sight-is-not") agreeing with आश्चर्याणि in नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन. भारत is सम्बोधन — it stands outside the action, a name to anchor the listener.

11.7.title: एकस्थम् — the whole cosmos in one place
11.7.body: एकस्थम् is a बहुव्रीहि ("that-which-stands-in-one-place") qualifying जगत् in नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन. कृत्स्नम् and स-चर-अचरम् are co-modifiers, the three adjectives forcing totality on a single noun.

11.7.title: मम देहे — सप्तमी as cosmic locus
11.7.body: देहे is सप्तमी एकवचन — locative not of place but of container. यत् च अन्यत् द्रष्टुम् इच्छसि is a relative clause: द्रष्टुम् is तुमुन्, इच्छसि लट् मध्यम एकवचन, यत् the नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया pronoun standing for any further object of desire.

11.8.title: न शक्यसे द्रष्टुम् — passive of capability
11.8.body: शक्यसे is लट् मध्यम पुरुष एकवचन passive of √शक्, with द्रष्टुम् as the dependent infinitive. The construction is grammatically passive ("thou-art-not-able-to-be-seen" reframed as "thou canst not see"), the human eye named स्वचक्षुषा in तृतीया.

11.8.title: दिव्यं चक्षुः ददामि — the gifted organ
11.8.body: ददामि is लट् उत्तम एकवचन of √दा. चक्षुः is नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन; दिव्यम् agrees. The grammar makes the divine eye a gift in the present tense, not a metaphor — and योगम् ऐश्वरम् is the द्वितीया object of the second पश्य.

11.9.title: एवम् उक्त्वा — absolutive shifting the speaker
11.9.body: उक्त्वा is the absolutive (क्त्वा) of √वच् — "having spoken thus" — and signals Sañjaya now takes the narrative reins. The finite verb दर्शयामास is लिट् (periphrastic perfect) प्रथम पुरुष एकवचन of causative √दृश्: "he caused to be seen."

11.9.title: महायोगेश्वरः हरिः — title before name
11.9.body: महा-योग-ईश्वरः is a कर्मधारय piling onto तत्पुरुष: "the great-lord-of-yoga," appositive to हरिः. राजन् is सम्बोधन addressed to Dhṛtarāṣṭra — the blind king is the listener for whom the whole cosmic vision is narrated.

11.10.title: अनेक-वक्त्र-नयनम् — the multiplicity prefix
11.10.body: अनेक- is the recurring prefix that defines this chapter: "many-mouths-and-eyes," a बहुव्रीहि agreeing with रूपम् understood from the previous verse. The whole verse is a string of accusative adjectives — no finite verb, only attribution.

11.10.title: उद्यत-आयुधम् — weapons raised, participle as adjective
11.10.body: उद्यत is past passive participle of उद् + √यम् ("raised up"), compounded with आयुध ("weapon") and दिव्य-अनेक- in a layered बहुव्रीहि. The participle never anchors — it stacks attributes on the rūpa Arjuna is being made to see.

11.11.title: दिव्य-माल्य-अम्बर-धरम् — wearer of garlands
11.11.body: धरम् is the agent-noun suffix ("bearer") in तत्पुरुष — "wearer-of-divine-garlands-and-garments." The compound is नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन agreeing with देवम्, continuing the verseless string of attributes.

11.11.title: विश्वतो-मुखम् — face on every side
11.11.body: विश्वतस् is the ablative-style adverb ("from all sides"), compounded with मुखम् in a बहुव्रीहि: "that-whose-face-is-everywhere." अनन्तम् and सर्वाश्चर्यमयम् close the catalogue, the mayaṭ suffix marking "made-of-all-wonder."

11.12.title: सूर्य-सहस्रस्य — the thousand-sun thought experiment
11.12.body: सूर्य-सहस्रस्य is षष्ठी एकवचन ("of a thousand suns"), the genitive of comparison. भवेत् is विधिलिङ् प्रथम एकवचन of √भू — the optative mood is essential: even the hypothesis is conditional, the simile cannot land on the indicative.

11.12.title: सदृशी स्यात् — the conditional simile
11.12.body: सदृशी is स्त्रीलिंग प्रथमा (agreeing with भाः, the f. noun "radiance"). स्यात् is again विधिलिङ् — "would-be similar." The whole verse is a contrafactual: even a thousand simultaneous sunrises would only approximate the महात्मा's light.

11.13.title: प्रविभक्तम् अनेकधा — divided manifold
11.13.body: प्रविभक्तम् is past passive participle of प्र + वि + √भज् ("divided apart"), qualifying जगत्. अनेकधा is the adverb-suffix ("in many ways"). The cosmos is simultaneously एकस्थम् (one-placed) and प्रविभक्तम् (many-divided) — the paradox the participle holds.

11.13.title: अपश्यत् — the verb that finally arrives
11.13.body: अपश्यत् is लङ् प्रथम पुरुष एकवचन of √दृश् — the imperfect with अ-augment and -त् ending. After three verses of stacked accusatives, the finite verb finally lands: Pāṇḍava saw. देव-देवस्य शरीरे is सप्तमी locative — within the body of the god-of-gods.

11.14.title: विस्मय-आविष्टः — possessed by wonder
11.14.body: आविष्टः is past passive participle of आ + √विश् ("entered into, possessed"), with विस्मय in compositional तृतीया sense. हृष्ट-रोमा is a बहुव्रीहि ("he-whose-body-hair-is-bristling") — the somatic register of awe in grammar.

11.14.title: प्रणम्य ... कृताञ्जलिः ... अभाषत — three actions, one finite
11.14.body: प्रणम्य is absolutive of प्र + √नम् ("having bowed"), कृताञ्जलिः a बहुव्रीहि ("he-with-folded-palms"), and अभाषत is लङ् आत्मनेपद प्रथम एकवचन of √भाष् — the single anchoring verb. Everything else orbits this "he spoke."

11.15.title: पश्यामि — Arjuna's refrain begins
11.15.body: पश्यामि is लट् उत्तम एकवचन of √दृश् ("I see"), the verb that will repeat through the hymn. Five accusatives follow: देवान्, भूत-विशेष-सङ्घान्, ब्रह्माणम्, ईशम्, ऋषीन्, उरगान् — the visible pantheon piled onto one finite verb.

11.15.title: कमल-आसन-स्थम् — locating Brahmā by posture
11.15.body: A tatpuruṣa-cum-बहुव्रीहि ("he-who-is-seated-on-the-lotus-seat") agreeing with ब्रह्माणम् in पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन. The cosmic vision is iconographically specific — Brahmā gets his lotus, Śiva his ईश, the ऋषिs and उरगs their dignity.

11.16.title: अनेक-बाहु-उदर-वक्त्र-नेत्रम् — fourfold multiplicity
11.16.body: A single बहुव्रीहि stringing four body-parts under one अनेक- prefix, agreeing with त्वाम् in पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन. Arms, bellies, mouths, eyes — the multiplicity is anatomical, not metaphorical.

11.16.title: न अन्तं न मध्यं न आदिम् — the triple negation
11.16.body: Three direct objects of पश्यामि, all पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन: end, middle, beginning. The grammar performs the limitlessness — every spatial coordinate denied in turn. विश्वेश्वर and विश्व-रूप are vocatives bracketing the negation.

11.17.title: किरीटिनम् गदिनम् चक्रिणम् — possessor suffix -इन्
11.17.body: Each adjective uses the -इन् (मतुप्-style) possessive: "crown-having, mace-having, discus-having," all पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन agreeing with त्वाम्. The familiar Viṣṇu iconography is fused into the कालानल form.

11.17.title: दुर्निरीक्ष्यम् — gerundive of impossibility
11.17.body: दुर्निरीक्ष्यम् is a कृत्य (gerundive) of दुस् + निर् + √ईक्ष् ("hard-to-look-at"), agreeing with त्वाम्. The grammar locks the impossibility into the form itself: not "I cannot see," but "thou-art-not-to-be-looked-at."

11.18.title: वेदितव्यम् — gerundive of obligation
11.18.body: वेदितव्यम् is कृत्य (तव्य-suffix) of √विद् ("that-which-is-to-be-known"), the supreme epistemic object. अक्षरम् परमम् agree with it in नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन — three predicate-nouns telescoped onto त्वम्.

11.18.title: शाश्वत-धर्म-गोप्ता — agent noun in -तृ
11.18.body: गोप्ता is the agent suffix (तृच्) on √गुप् ("protector"), compounded with शाश्वत-धर्म in तत्पुरुष: "protector-of-eternal-धर्म." सनातनः, पुरुषः, अव्ययः stack as predicates, all पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with त्वम्.

11.19.title: अनादि-मध्य-अन्तम् — beginningless-middle-endless
11.19.body: A triple negative बहुव्रीहि ("that-whose-beginning-middle-end-is-not"), agreeing with त्वाम्. The same negation as 11.16, now compacted into a single compound. अनन्त-वीर्यम् and अनन्त-बाहुम् continue the अनन्त- chain.

11.19.title: विश्वम् तपन्तम् — present participle as adjective
11.19.body: तपन्तम् is the शतृ present participle of √तप् ("scorching"), agreeing with त्वाम् in पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन. स्व-तेजसा is तृतीया एकवचन — the instrument of scorching is the form's own light, not an external sun.

11.20.title: द्यावा-पृथिव्योः — Vedic dual genitive
11.20.body: द्यावा-पृथिव्योः is षष्ठी द्विवचन of the द्वन्द्व "heaven-and-earth," archaic and Vedic. व्याप्तम् is past passive participle of वि + √आप्, agreeing with अन्तरम् in नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन — the space between is pervaded.

11.20.title: लोक-त्रयम् प्रव्यथितम् — the three worlds shaken
11.20.body: लोक-त्रयम् is नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन ("the triad-of-worlds"), with प्रव्यथितम् past passive participle of प्र + वि + √व्यथ् as predicate. दृष्ट्वा is absolutive of √दृश् — having-seen, the three worlds shudder.

11.21.title: विशन्ति — present plural entering the form
11.21.body: विशन्ति is लट् प्रथम बहुवचन of √विश् ("they enter"), the verb that will return in 11.27, 11.28, 11.29 with grimmer cargo. Here the entrants are सुर-सङ्घाः — divine companies. भीताः is past passive participle modifying केचित् ("some, terrified").

11.21.title: स्वस्ति इति उक्त्वा — quoting the cry
11.21.body: उक्त्वा is absolutive of √वच्, with the literal cry स्वस्ति ("hail!") inside the इति-marker. महर्षि-सिद्ध-सङ्घाः is the subject of स्तुवन्ति (लट् प्रथम बहुवचन of √स्तु), with पुष्कलाभिः स्तुतिभिः in तृतीया बहुवचन — the instrument of praise.

11.22.title: वीक्षन्ते — आत्मनेपद of beholding
11.22.body: वीक्षन्ते is लट् प्रथम बहुवचन आत्मनेपद of वि + √ईक्ष् ("they behold"). The catalogued subject is a great द्वन्द्व: रुद्र-आदित्याः, वसवः, साध्याः, विश्वे, अश्विनौ, मरुतः, ऊष्मपाः, gandharva-yakṣa-asura-siddha-saṅghāḥ — every divine class staring.

11.22.title: विस्मिताः — past participle of wonder
11.22.body: विस्मिताः is past passive participle of वि + √स्मि ("astonished"), पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन, agreeing with सर्वे. The participle is predicative: not the doers of action but the bearers of a state — even the gods are reduced to spectators.

11.23.title: बहु- prefix replaces अनेक-
11.23.body: Where 11.16 used अनेक-, this verse switches to बहु- in बहु-वक्त्र-नेत्रम्, बहु-बाहु-ऊरु-पादम्, बहु-उदरम्, बहु-दंष्ट्रा-करालम् — four parallel बहुव्रीहि compounds qualifying रूपम् in नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन.

11.23.title: प्रव्यथिताः तथा अहम् — community of fear
11.23.body: प्रव्यथिताः is past passive participle of प्र + वि + √व्यथ् in पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन, agreeing with लोकाः. The तथा अहम् at the end collapses Arjuna into the same predicate — he too is shaken, no different from the worlds he sees being shaken.

11.24.title: नभः-स्पृशम् — touching-the-sky compound
11.24.body: नभः-स्पृशम् is उपपद तत्पुरुष: स्पृश् ("touching") with नभः as its object, fused as an adjective. व्यात्त-आननम् uses past passive participle व्यात्त ("opened wide") of वि + √आ-दा. Both qualify the form.

11.24.title: धृतिं न विन्दामि — losing composure
11.24.body: विन्दामि is लट् उत्तम एकवचन of √विद् (Gaṇa 6, "to find"). धृतिम् and शमम् are द्वितीया एकवचन objects — fortitude and peace, neither found. प्रव्यथित-अन्तर-आत्मा is a बहुव्रीहि ("he-whose-inner-self-is-shaken") describing Arjuna himself.

11.25.title: काल-अनल-सन्निभानि — fire-of-time simile
11.25.body: सन्निभानि is नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन ("resembling"), with काल-अनल in तत्पुरुष ("fire-of-time"). The simile prefigures Krishna's own self-declaration in 11.32. दंष्ट्रा-करालानि is a बहुव्रीहि ("fang-frightful") qualifying मुखानि.

11.25.title: दिशो न जाने — losing the directions
11.25.body: जाने is लट् उत्तम एकवचन आत्मनेपद of √ज्ञा (irregular, "I know"). दिशः is स्त्रीलिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन — the cardinal points themselves are lost. प्रसीद is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of प्र + √सद्: the chapter's recurring imperative "be gracious."

11.26.title: अमी ... पुत्राः — the demonstrative recognition
11.26.body: अमी is पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन of the deictic अदस् ("those-yonder") — Arjuna sees his cousins enter the mouths. No finite verb in this pāda; the verb विशन्ति arrives only in 11.27, making 11.26–27 a single grammatical breath.

11.26.title: अवनिपाल-सङ्घैः — kings as instrumental
11.26.body: सहैव अवनिपाल-सङ्घैः is सह + तृतीया बहुवचन — "together with the king-protector-companies." The compound अस्मदीयैः योध-मुख्यैः balances on "our side" too: Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Karṇa, all funneling into the same maw.

11.27.title: विशन्ति — the finite verb 11.26 was waiting for
11.27.body: विशन्ति is लट् प्रथम बहुवचन of √विश्. वक्त्राणि is नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन — direct object, "into the mouths." त्वरमाणाः is present आत्मनेपद participle (शानच्) of √त्वर्ः "hurrying."

11.27.title: चूर्णितैः उत्तम-अङ्गैः — instrumental of crushed heads
11.27.body: चूर्णितैः is past passive participle of √चूर्ण in तृतीया बहुवचन, agreeing with उत्तम-अङ्गैः ("highest-limbs" = heads). सन्दृश्यन्ते is लट् प्रथम बहुवचन passive of सम् + √दृश् — "they are seen," the warriors visible only as fragments.

11.28.title: यथा ... तथा — the river simile
11.28.body: यथा-तथा is the canonical similitude pair. The vehicle: नदीनां बहवः अम्बु-वेगाः (षष्ठी + प्रथमा बहुवचन, "the many water-currents of rivers") rushing समुद्रम् एव अभिमुखाः (पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन).

11.28.title: द्रवन्ति → विशन्ति — current to mouth
11.28.body: द्रवन्ति (लट् प्रथम बहुवचन of √द्रु, "they run") in the simile becomes विशन्ति in the tenor. नर-लोक-वीराः (पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन) are the human-world-heroes; अभिविज्वलन्ति is present participle (शतृ) of अभि + वि + √ज्वल्, agreeing with वक्त्राणि — "the blazing mouths."

11.29.title: पतङ्ग-simile — moths into the flame
11.29.body: Second simile-pair: पतङ्गाः (पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन, "moths") with समृद्ध-वेगाः ("with-full-speed," बहुव्रीहि) rush ज्वलनम् (पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन, "the blaze") for नाशाय (चतुर्थी एकवचन of purpose, "unto destruction").

11.29.title: नाशाय — चतुर्थी of telos
11.29.body: नाशाय is the dative-of-purpose used twice — once for moths, once for लोकाः. The grammar fuses cause and direction: they enter not into but for destruction. लोकाः and पतङ्गाः share the same case, number, fate.

11.30.title: लेलिह्यसे — frequentative of licking
11.30.body: लेलिह्यसे is the यङ् (intensive/frequentative) of √लिह्, लट् मध्यम एकवचन आत्मनेपद — "thou keepest licking again and again." The reduplicated stem लेलिह्य- enacts the repeated motion grammatically.

11.30.title: ग्रसमानः — present participle as predicate
11.30.body: ग्रसमानः is शानच् present participle of √ग्रस् ("devouring"), पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with the subject त्वम् of लेलिह्यसे. The finite verb is intensive-frequentative; the participle is durative — both stack continuous violence.

11.31.title: आख्याहि — imperative of disclosure
11.31.body: आख्याहि is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of आ + √ख्या ("declare"). कः भवान् is the polite interrogative: "who-are-you?" — भवान् is the honorific pronoun, पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन. उग्र-रूपः is a बहुव्रीहि predicate ("of-terrible-form").

11.31.title: तव प्रवृत्तिम् न प्रजानामि — not knowing the intent
11.31.body: प्रजानामि is लट् उत्तम एकवचन of प्र + √ज्ञा. प्रवृत्तिम् is स्त्रीलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन ("engagement, intent, motion-forth"). The question is not who but why — the form is visible, the purpose hidden.

11.32.title: कालः अस्मि — the self-definition
11.32.body: कालः is पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन, अस्मि लट् उत्तम एकवचन of √अस् — the bluntest copula in the Gītā. लोक-क्षय-कृत् is उपपद तत्पुरुष ("world-destruction-doer"), प्रवृद्धः past passive participle of प्र + √वृध् ("swollen, grown forth").

11.32.title: ऋते अपि त्वाम् — even-without-you
11.32.body: ऋते ("without/except") here governs the द्वितीया त्वाम् — "even without thee"; ऋते optionally takes द्वितीया or पञ्चमी. भविष्यन्ति is लृट् प्रथम बहुवचन of √भू, spotted by the -ष्य- infix — "they will not be." अवस्थिताः is past passive participle of अव + √स्था, agreeing with योधाः.

11.33.title: उत्तिष्ठ — the imperative echoing 2.3
11.33.body: उत्तिष्ठ is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of उद् + √स्था — the same command Krishna gave in 2.3 (त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ). The chapter's metaphysical climax circles back to the original ethical command: rise. यशः लभस्व is लोट् आत्मनेपद of √लभ्.

11.33.title: निमित्त-मात्रं भव — the instrument doctrine
11.33.body: भव is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of √भू ("become"). निमित्त-मात्रम् is नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन ("merely-an-instrument"), the predicate nominative. मया एव एते निहताः पूर्वम् एव — निहताः past passive participle, the killing already accomplished in tense.

11.34.title: मया हतान् त्वं जहि — kill the already-killed
11.34.body: हतान् is past passive participle of √हन् in पुल्लिंग द्वितीया बहुवचन, agreeing with the named warriors. जहि is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of √हन्. The grammar fuses past and future: "slay (now) those-slain (by me already)."

11.34.title: जेतासि — periphrastic future tense
11.34.body: जेतासि is लुट् (periphrastic future, distinct from लृट्) मध्यम एकवचन of √जि — "thou shalt be victor." मा व्यथिष्ठाः is लुङ् (aorist) with मा-prohibition, मध्यम एकवचन आत्मनेपद of √व्यथ् — "do not tremble." Two future-domain forms bracket the command.

11.35.title: एतत् श्रुत्वा — absolutive of hearing
11.35.body: श्रुत्वा is absolutive of √श्रु ("having heard"), with एतत् वचनम् as its accusative object. वेपमानः is शानच् present participle of √वेप् ("trembling"), agreeing with किरीटी — Arjuna identified by his crown alone.

11.35.title: स-गद्गदम् ... भीत-भीतः — adverbial doubling of fear
11.35.body: स-गद्गदम् is an adverb ("with-stammering") of आह — the finite verb, लिट् प्रथम एकवचन of √अह् ("he said"). भीत-भीतः is reduplicated past passive participle ("frightened-frightened") — the doubling is intensive: utterly afraid.

11.36.title: स्थाने — adverbial "fittingly"
11.36.body: स्थाने is सप्तमी एकवचन of स्थान used adverbially: "it is appropriate that..." The whole verse hangs from this opening word. प्रहृष्यति and अनुरज्यते are लट् प्रथम एकवचन — the world rejoices and is enamoured.

11.36.title: द्रवन्ति — Rakṣasas fleeing
11.36.body: द्रवन्ति is लट् प्रथम बहुवचन of √द्रु — the same verb used for rivers in 11.28, now for rākṣasas. रक्षांसि is नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन subject, भीतानि past passive participle predicate — they-are-frightened, they run.

11.37.title: कस्मात् न नमेरन् — विधिलिङ् of obligation
11.37.body: नमेरन् is विधिलिङ् प्रथम बहुवचन आत्मनेपद of √नम् — "why-should-they-not-bow." कस्मात् is पञ्चमी एकवचन interrogative ("for-what-reason"). The mood encodes the rhetorical "of course they should."

11.37.title: ब्रह्मणः अपि आदि-कर्त्रे — even Brahmā's first cause
11.37.body: गरीयसे and आदि-कर्त्रे are चतुर्थी एकवचन (datives governed by नमेरन्). ब्रह्मणः is षष्ठी एकवचन depending on आदि-कर्त्रे — "first-creator even of Brahmā." सत् असत् तत् परम् stacks predicates of अक्षरम्.

11.38.title: त्वम् आदि-देवः — string of predicates
11.38.body: Five predicates apposed to त्वम् in पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन: आदि-देवः, पुरुषः, पुराणः, परं निधानम्, परम् धाम. वेत्ता and वेद्यम् are paired agent-noun (तृच्) and gerundive — knower and known both.

11.38.title: त्वया ततं विश्वम् — pervaded by thee
11.38.body: ततम् is past passive participle of √तन् ("stretched, pervaded"), agreeing with विश्वम् in नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन. त्वया is तृतीया एकवचन (the agent). अनन्त-रूप is सम्बोधन — the form whose limits the participle has just denied.

11.39.title: Vedic-deity catalogue — predicates of त्वम्
11.39.body: वायुः, यमः, अग्निः, वरुणः, शशाङ्कः, प्रजापतिः, प्रपितामहः — seven nominatives apposed to त्वम्. No finite verb; अस्ति is implicit. The Vedic pantheon collapses into one referent through bare apposition.

11.39.title: सहस्र-कृत्वः — adverb of frequency
11.39.body: सहस्र-कृत्वः is the -कृत्वस् adverbial suffix ("a thousand times"). नमः नमः ते अस्तु — नमः is नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन, अस्तु लोट् प्रथम एकवचन of √अस् — "let there be salutation." The repetition is grammatically encoded, not merely rhetorical.

11.40.title: नमः पुरस्तात् अथ पृष्ठतः — bowing in all directions
11.40.body: पुरस्तात्, पृष्ठतः, सर्वतः — three -तस् ablative-style adverbs of direction. The salutation is spatial as well as repeated. सर्व is सम्बोधन ("O All-one") — the addressee literally is every direction at once.

11.40.title: ततः असि सर्वः — therefore thou art all
11.40.body: असि is लट् मध्यम एकवचन of √अस्. समाप्नोषि is लट् मध्यम एकवचन of सम् + √आप् — "thou fully obtainest/pervadest." ततः ("therefore") makes the apposition deductive: pervading-everything implies being-everything.

11.41.title: सख इति मत्वा — taking-for-friend
11.41.body: मत्वा is absolutive of √मन् ("having-thought"), with the predicative सखा inside the इति-quote — "having considered (you) a friend." प्रसभम् is adverbial ("impetuously"). उक्तम् is past passive participle as predicate of the implicit relative.

11.41.title: अजानता — present participle in तृतीया
11.41.body: अजानता is शतृ negative present participle of √ज्ञा, in तृतीया एकवचन — "by (me) not-knowing." The participle absorbs the agent's ignorance into a single instrumental form. प्रमादात् and प्रणयेन (पञ्चमी and तृतीया) name the two causes: heedlessness and affection.

11.42.title: अवहास-अर्थम् — purpose-compound
11.42.body: अवहास-अर्थम् is a तत्पुरुष used adverbially ("for-the-sake-of-jest"). असत्-कृतः is पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन past passive participle ("treated-badly"), agreeing with the implicit त्वम्. The grammar names the disrespect and the apology in one breath.

11.42.title: क्षामये त्वाम् — causative request for forgiveness
11.42.body: क्षामये is लट् उत्तम एकवचन of the causative of √क्षम् — "I beg-you-to-forgive." त्वाम् is द्वितीया एकवचन direct object. The whole catalogue (विहार-शय्या-आसन-भोजनेषु — सप्तमी बहुवचन of contexts) is folded under one apology.

11.43.title: पिता असि लोकस्य — predicative भू-substitute
11.43.body: पिता is पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन — predicate nominative with असि (√अस्). चर-अचरस्य is षष्ठी एकवचन ("of moving-and-unmoving") — the totality of beings folded under a single father-noun.

11.43.title: त्वत्-समः ... अभ्यधिकः कुतः अन्यः — comparative escalation
11.43.body: त्वत्-समः is a तत्पुरुष ("equal-to-thee") in पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन. अभ्यधिकः is comparative-style adjective ("greater"). कुतः अन्यः is ablative-adverb plus pronoun — "how-from-elsewhere?" The grammar performs incomparability.

11.44.title: प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायम् — chained absolutives
11.44.body: प्रणम्य ("having-bowed") and प्रणिधाय ("having-laid-down") are absolutives of प्र + √नम् and प्र + नि + √धा. कायम् is पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन — body laid prostrate. Both absolutives govern the finite प्रसादये.

11.44.title: पिता इव सखा इव प्रियः प्रियायाः — three relational similes
11.44.body: Three इव similes piled: father-son, friend-friend, lover-beloved. प्रसादये is लट् उत्तम एकवचन आत्मनेपद of the causative of प्र + √सद् — "I propitiate." अर्हसि सोढुम् — अर्हसि लट् मध्यम एकवचन with तुमुन् infinitive सोढुम् of √सह् ("to-bear").

11.45.title: अदृष्ट-पूर्वम् — "never-seen-before"
11.45.body: अदृष्ट-पूर्वम् is a बहुव्रीहि ("that-whose-prior-sight-is-not") used adverbially-ish here as नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन. हृषितः अस्मि is past passive participle ("I-am-thrilled") of √हृष् — passive perfect with auxiliary.

11.45.title: तत् एव मे रूपं दर्शय — show that-very form
11.45.body: दर्शय is causative लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of √दृश् — "cause-to-be-shown." तत् एव is emphatic — "that very (former) form," not this terrifying one. The vocatives देवेश and जगन्निवास mark formal address.

11.46.title: किरीटिनम् गदिनम् चक्र-हस्तम् — the four-armed form requested
11.46.body: Three accusatives in पुल्लिंग द्वितीया एकवचन, all qualifying त्वाम् — crown-having, mace-having, discus-in-hand. चक्र-हस्तम् is a बहुव्रीहि ("discus-hand"). इच्छामि द्रष्टुम् is लट् + तुमुन् — "I-wish-to-see.".

11.46.title: भव — return-to-form imperative
11.46.body: भव is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of √भू — "become." चतुर्भुजेन रूपेण is तृतीया एकवचन (instrumental of manner). सहस्र-बाहो and विश्व-मूर्ते are vocatives — even the request acknowledges the cosmic form just witnessed.

11.47.title: मया प्रसन्नेन — grace as तृतीया
11.47.body: मया is तृतीया एकवचन of the agent, with प्रसन्नेन (past passive participle of प्र + √सद्) agreeing — "by-me-being-graced." दर्शितम् is past passive participle of causative √दृश् — "shown," agreeing with रूपम् (नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन).

11.47.title: त्वत्-अन्येन न दृष्ट-पूर्वम् — none other has seen
11.47.body: त्वत्-अन्येन is तृतीया एकवचन ("by-other-than-thee"). दृष्ट-पूर्वम् is the past-passive-participle-plus-पूर्व बहुव्रीहि ("of-which-prior-sight-is-not"). The grammar elevates Arjuna by absolute exclusion: not negation in general, but negation of every alternative seer.

11.48.title: न ... न ... न — fourfold negation of means
11.48.body: वेद-यज्ञ-अध्ययनैः, दानैः, क्रियाभिः, तपोभिः उग्रैः — four तृतीया बहुवचन nouns of religious means, each negated. The grammar bars every conventional path. शक्यः is the gerundive predicate ("capable-of-being-seen").

11.48.title: द्रष्टुम् त्वत्-अन्येन — none-but-thee
11.48.body: द्रष्टुम् is तुमुन् of √दृश्. त्वत्-अन्येन repeats from 11.47 — "by-other-than-thee." कुरु-प्रवीर is सम्बोधन. The exclusivity is sealed across two verses: not means, not other seers — only this revealed vision.

11.49.title: मा ते व्यथा — niṣedha without finite verb
11.49.body: मा is the prohibitive particle. व्यथा (स्त्रीलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन, "trembling") and विमूढ-भावः (पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन, "deluded-state") are nominatives — the verbal भू understood: "let-there-not-be (for) thee."

11.49.title: व्यपेत-भीः प्रीत-मनाः — paired बहुव्रीहि compounds
11.49.body: व्यपेत-भीः is "he-whose-fear-has-departed," with past passive participle व्यपेत of वि + अप + √इ. प्रीत-मनाः is "he-whose-mind-is-pleased" (past passive participle of √प्री). Both पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन, predicates of त्वम्. प्रपश्य is लोट् मध्यम एकवचन of प्र + √दृश्.

11.50.title: इति ... उक्त्वा — Sañjaya returns
11.50.body: उक्त्वा is absolutive of √वच् — Sañjaya's narrative frame reasserts. वासुदेवः ... दर्शयामास भूयः — दर्शयामास is लिट् (periphrastic perfect) of causative √दृश्, प्रथम एकवचन: "he-caused-to-be-shown again."

11.50.title: सौम्य-वपुः — beautiful-bodied
11.50.body: सौम्य-वपुः is a बहुव्रीहि ("he-whose-form-is-gentle"), पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन agreeing with महात्मा. भूत्वा is absolutive of √भू ("having-become"). आश्वासयामास is लिट् of causative आ + √श्वस् ("caused to be reassured") — Krishna twice consoles, in form and in word.

11.51.title: दृष्ट्वा इदम् मानुषं रूपम् — back to the human form
11.51.body: दृष्ट्वा is absolutive of √दृश्. इदम् मानुषम् रूपम् is नपुंसकलिंग द्वितीया एकवचन — "this human form," finally restored. सौम्यम् agrees as adjective. जनार्दन is सम्बोधन.

11.51.title: संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः — three predicates of recovery
11.51.body: संवृत्तः is past passive participle of सम् + √वृत् — "become / settled." सचेताः is a बहुव्रीहि ("he-with-his-wits"). प्रकृतिं गतः is "gone-to-(his)-own-nature" — गतः past passive participle of √गम्. The grammar reassembles Arjuna's composure piece by piece.

11.52.title: सु-दुर्-दर्शम् — doubly-hard-to-see
11.52.body: सु-दुर्-दर्शम् stacks two intensifying prefixes onto the gerundive दर्शम् ("to-be-seen") of √दृश् — "very-hard-to-see." The form is नपुंसकलिंग प्रथमा एकवचन predicate of रूपम्. दृष्टवान् असि is the periphrastic perfect with क्तवतु: "thou-hast-seen."

11.52.title: देवाः अपि ... दर्शन-काङ्क्षिणः — even gods crave
11.52.body: दर्शन-काङ्क्षिणः is पुल्लिंग प्रथमा बहुवचन — "sight-cravers," with -इन् possessive suffix. The compound is उपपद तत्पुरुष. नित्यम् is adverbial. Arjuna's privilege is calibrated against the gods' eternal longing — even they only क्षाङ्क्षन्ति.

11.53.title: न वेदैः न तपसा न दानेन न इज्यया — exhaustive negation
11.53.body: Four तृतीया (instrumental) nouns of religious means, each blocked by न: Vedas, austerity, gift, sacrifice. शक्यः ... द्रष्टुम् pairs gerundive शक्यः (पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन) with तुमुन् द्रष्टुम् — capable-of-being-seen.

11.53.title: दृष्टवान् असि ... यथा — comparative clause
11.53.body: दृष्टवान् is the क्तवतु past active participle of √दृश्, with auxiliary असि — "thou-hast-seen." यथा here is correlative — "in the manner (thou hast seen)." The verse sets up the contrast that 11.54 will resolve: not by these, but by bhakti.

11.54.title: भक्त्या तु अनन्यया — instrumental of exclusive devotion
11.54.body: भक्त्या is तृतीया एकवचन of भक्ति; अनन्यया agrees ("by-undeflecting"). तु contrasts with the negated instrumentals of 11.53. शक्यः अहम् ... एवंविधः is the predicate frame: I-of-this-kind am capable-(of-being).

11.54.title: ज्ञातुम् द्रष्टुम् प्रवेष्टुम् — triple infinitive ladder
11.54.body: Three तुमुन् infinitives of √ज्ञा, √दृश्, प्र + √विश् — "to-know, to-see, to-enter." The order is pedagogical: cognition, vision, union. तत्त्वेन is तृतीया एकवचन ("in-truth, really") — qualifying all three. Only bhakti makes the entire ladder climbable.

11.55.title: मत्-कर्म-कृत् मत्-परमः मद्-भक्तः — three bahuvrīhis on "me"
11.55.body: Three compounds with मद्/मत् as initial member: "my-work-doer, me-supreme-having, my-devotee." All पुल्लिंग प्रथमा एकवचन, qualifying यः. The grammar concentrates everything on a single pronoun-stem.

11.55.title: सः माम् एति — the final formula
11.55.body: एति is लट् प्रथम एकवचन of √इ ("goes/comes"). माम् is द्वितीया एकवचन direct object. सङ्ग-वर्जितः is past passive participle ("attachment-shunned"); निर्वैरः a बहुव्रीहि ("he-without-enmity"). पाण्डव is सम्बोधन. The chapter that began with कालः अस्मि ends with माम् एति: time devours, but the bhakta reaches.
